title: Mira's Draining Day
cover_prompt: A small girl with damp curls sits in a deep porcelain tub, bubbles clinging to her shoulders as the water swirls down the drain. The room glows with warm lamplight, steam curling like dreams above the water.
lyrics:
[verse]
What a day, what a day
Water splashed and soap would sting
Towel so warm, it wrapped away
All the noise that stung my skin

[verse]
What a day, what a day
Loudest voices, sharpest words
Toys all scattered, rules all blurred
I just wanted to be heard

[chorus]
The day is done, the day is done
Bubbles pop and fade to none
Drain the tub, the fight is gone
The day is done, the day is done

[verse]
What a day, what a day
Pajamas soft, the lights burn low
Stories whispered, not too slow
Now the stars will watch me go

[chorus]
The day is done, the day is done
Bubbles pop and fade to none
Drain the tub, the fight is gone
The day is done, the day is done

[chorus]
The day is done, the day is done
Bubbles pop and fade to none
Drain the tub, the fight is gone
The day is done, and the day is done
style_prompt: Intimate domestic lullaby, warm female vocal, soft acoustic guitar, 64 BPM, cozy, familiar, conversational, almost speaking melodically, not performative, kitchen-to-bedroom energy, end of day warmth, fingerpicked acoustic guitar accompaniment

character.name: Mira
character.identity: A spirited little girl who finds peace in her evening bath after a long, loud day.
character.special: She lets the water carry away her frustrations, leaving only quiet behind.

about: A soothing lullaby for children who struggle to let go of the day’s chaos. The rhythmic draining of the bath water mirrors the release of tension, helping young minds find closure. The repeated chorus gently reinforces that the day is over, making it easier to drift into sleep.
